zero_slop_policy.statement: NO AI SLOP ALLOWED AT ALL - ZERO TOLERANCE TO SLOP AND HALLUCINATIONS
zero_slop_policy.strict_compliance: Mandatory. Every claim must be project-specific, evidence-backed, trace-linked, reviewer-challenged, and free of unsupported invention.
zero_slop_policy.missing_evidence_rule: If evidence is missing, mark it as an assumption, risk, open question, or failure.

reviewer_hint: Product Expert, QA Lead, Domain Expert

pass_policy.failed_points_require: fix evidence or owner-approved residual risk with expiry and revalidation trigger

artifact_level_rubric.name: Artifact identity and lifecycle state
artifact_level_rubric.intent: Acceptance Criteria and Scenarios declares artifact ID, version, lifecycle phase, status, owner, approval authority, control graph node, and work ledger item.

artifact_level_rubric.name: Source intent and authorization
artifact_level_rubric.intent: Acceptance Criteria and Scenarios ties every material claim to customer answers, approved assumptions, source transcripts, issue records, or change requests.

artifact_level_rubric.name: Scope boundary and tailoring
artifact_level_rubric.intent: Acceptance Criteria and Scenarios defines in scope, out of scope, deferred items, tailoring rationale, and combination/split rationale when applicable.

artifact_level_rubric.name: Artifact-specific focus: user scenarios
artifact_level_rubric.intent: Acceptance Criteria and Scenarios addresses `user scenarios` with enough specificity to guide downstream work, review, and verification.

artifact_level_rubric.name: Artifact-specific focus: acceptance criteria
artifact_level_rubric.intent: Acceptance Criteria and Scenarios addresses `acceptance criteria` with enough specificity to guide downstream work, review, and verification.

artifact_level_rubric.name: Artifact-specific focus: negative cases
artifact_level_rubric.intent: Acceptance Criteria and Scenarios addresses `negative cases` with enough specificity to guide downstream work, review, and verification.

artifact_level_rubric.name: Artifact-specific focus: holdouts
artifact_level_rubric.intent: Acceptance Criteria and Scenarios addresses `holdouts` with enough specificity to guide downstream work, review, and verification.

artifact_level_rubric.name: Artifact-specific focus: journey coverage
artifact_level_rubric.intent: Acceptance Criteria and Scenarios addresses `journey coverage` with enough specificity to guide downstream work, review, and verification.

artifact_level_rubric.name: Family theme coverage: downstream allocation
artifact_level_rubric.intent: Acceptance Criteria and Scenarios handles `downstream allocation` according to its artifact family obligations.

artifact_level_rubric.name: Standards and method mapping
artifact_level_rubric.intent: Acceptance Criteria and Scenarios maps named standards and methods to concrete sections, evidence, or justified exclusions.

artifact_level_rubric.name: Bidirectional traceability
artifact_level_rubric.intent: Acceptance Criteria and Scenarios supports forward and reverse trace across intent, requirements, design, implementation, tests, operations, risks, and decisions as applicable.

artifact_level_rubric.name: Decision rationale and alternatives
artifact_level_rubric.intent: Acceptance Criteria and Scenarios records material decisions, alternatives considered, rationale, consequences, and reversal triggers.

artifact_level_rubric.name: Risk, assumption, and residual-risk handling
artifact_level_rubric.intent: Acceptance Criteria and Scenarios records risks, assumptions, constraints, mitigation, owner, expiry, and revalidation trigger.

artifact_level_rubric.name: Verification and acceptance evidence
artifact_level_rubric.intent: Acceptance Criteria and Scenarios defines how its claims will be verified and links to test evidence, review evidence, holdouts, or operational proof.

artifact_level_rubric.name: Artifact-specific critic panel instantiated
artifact_level_rubric.intent: Acceptance Criteria and Scenarios has a linked Artifact Review Panel Record with content authority, governance/trace, and verification/handoff critics.

artifact_level_rubric.name: Handoff and future resumption
artifact_level_rubric.intent: Acceptance Criteria and Scenarios includes human handoff, agent handoff, next action, and re-entry triggers so work can continue without hidden context.

artifact_level_rubric.name: Quality certificate readiness
artifact_level_rubric.intent: Acceptance Criteria and Scenarios is ready for refinery gate and certificate only when failed rubric points have fixes or accepted residual risk.

artifact_level_rubric.name: No template residue or unsupported claims
artifact_level_rubric.intent: Acceptance Criteria and Scenarios contains no placeholder text, fictional examples as real evidence, generic filler, or claims beyond evidence.

critic_seat_rubrics.mandate: validates user goals, business rules, edge scenarios, and prioritization.

checks.name: Persona mandate declared
checks.intent: The REQ-004-C1 Product Scenario Critic critic declares the elite persona, decision rights, non-negotiables, red flags, and evidence required for `Acceptance Criteria and Scenarios`.

checks.name: Mandate-specific completeness
checks.intent: The critic verifies its mandate: validates user goals, business rules, edge scenarios, and prioritization.

checks.name: Artifact focus challenge
checks.intent: The critic inspects `user scenarios` and challenges whether it is concrete, evidenced, and useful.

checks.name: Secondary focus challenge
checks.intent: The critic inspects `acceptance criteria` for completeness, trace, and downstream usability.

checks.name: Tertiary focus challenge
checks.intent: The critic inspects `negative cases` for risk, edge cases, and acceptance impact.

checks.name: Standards fit
checks.intent: The critic confirms `BDD/TDD, dark factory validation` is applied through concrete artifact content, not name-dropping.

checks.name: Trace closure
checks.intent: The critic verifies source, forward, reverse, and evidence links relevant to the content authority lens.

checks.name: Evidence sufficiency
checks.intent: The critic verifies that evidence is inspectable, current, project-specific, and not merely a template.

checks.name: Risk and residual-risk treatment
checks.intent: The critic verifies risks in its lane have owner, mitigation, acceptance state, expiry, and revalidation triggers.

checks.name: Adversarial cross-critique
checks.intent: The critic challenges at least one assumption from another critic seat and records whether it survived scrutiny.

checks.name: Downstream usability
checks.intent: The critic verifies that downstream artifacts, code, tests, operations, or handoff users can act on this artifact.

checks.name: Iteration and change control
checks.intent: The critic checks whether artifact changes affect scope, token budget, approvals, or baseline state.

checks.name: Failure and edge handling
checks.intent: The critic verifies negative cases, failure modes, contradiction handling, or operational exceptions appropriate to `Acceptance Criteria and Scenarios`.

checks.name: Pass/fail vote integrity
checks.intent: The critic's score, rationale, failed points, fixes, and residual risks are explicit and consistent.

checks.name: Specialist escalation judgment
checks.intent: The critic decides whether additional specialists are required because artifact risk exceeds the default three-seat panel.
